reviews.author: Earth Wanderer
reviews.text: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ A Spiritual City: Bai Dinh Pagoda is Unforgettable Bai Dinh Pagoda is not just a temple; it is a monumental testament to Vietnamese Buddhism and a genuinely awe-inspiring spiritual city. I cannot recommend this destination enough—it is a solid five-star experience that seamlessly blends immense scale with profound tranquility. The moment you arrive, the sheer size and grandeur of the New Pagoda complex are breathtaking. It holds numerous Asian and Vietnamese records, including the Largest Gold plated Buddha Statue and the Longest Corridor of Arhat Statues (featuring 500 beautifully unique figures!). Walking along this corridor, you feel a deep sense of history and spiritual contemplation. What makes it so special is the blend of the old and the new. While the new complex is magnificent, you can also explore the Ancient Bai Dinh Pagoda, hidden on the hillside. This older, more intimate space—including sacred caves and grottoes—offers a quiet, thousand-year-old connection to the roots of Vietnamese Buddhist worship. The architecture is majestic, using local materials and featuring traditional curved roofs, all set against the stunning backdrop of the Ninh Binh limestone mountains. The atmosphere is peaceful and meditative, making it a perfect place for reflection, regardless of your faith. Bai Dinh Pagoda is a true pilgrimage site and a must-see cultural wonder in Vietnam. Be sure to dedicate at least half a day to fully appreciate its immense beauty and scale.

reviews.author: Tala Pinoy
reviews.text: A vast spiritual complex will be difficult to complete without a knowledgeable guide and the EV tram. With that said, the visiting sequence should be getting the ticket(s) at ticket building near parking lot, select menu 3 (Hanh Trinh Vui Khoe) d600k/2 pak, then hop on the tram. There aren’t any restaurant at each site, nor place to sit after long walk. Thus, with an English speaking guide d500k and your whole trip will be more enjoyable. The park map (layout & site orientation map) sucks or none existence thus the guide is a must. The attractions are not in any particular order here; but we visit Bao Thao (Stupa) with 360deg commanding views, Yuan-Yin Hall which contains bodhisatta with many arms and eyes to help many people at once. Flanking the many hands bodhisatta, 2 large black Buddha statues carved out from single tree are impressively tall. Then there is three periods temple that worships 3 identical Buddha statues which represent the past, present, and future. There is a long corridor lined with 500 green stone arhats (disciples) that turn black from many visitor touching. Lunch can be had by taking the tram back to their main restaurant (our guide also facilitates the waitress for ordering, or else it would take a bit of headache getting the attention of the waitress). Be prepared to pay more than d500k for merely 3 dishes of your choice. Like Disneyland, treat this Buddha-themed complex more of casual introduction to Buddhism faith. The monks will come early in the morning at 5am to gong the bells, and again at 5pm. Monks doesn’t stay inside the complex like other ancient temple. Also this faith dictates liberation from suffering and cycle of rebirth by cultivating self wisdom, ethical conduct, and mental discipline rather blind faith in creator god statues. Thus enjoy these as an expression of the faith rather than statue that give you your life meaning.

reviews.author: Zhang Qingye
reviews.text: Visited Bai Dinh Pagoda alone on 21 January 2026, and even with the rainy weather, it was a calm and meaningful experience. The complex is vast and impressive, with grand architecture, long corridors, and a strong sense of peace throughout. Walking around in the rain made the place feel quieter and more reflective, with fewer crowds than usual. Despite the size of the pagoda, it was well organised, with clear paths and signage, making it easy to explore at my own pace. The mist and light rain added a serene atmosphere, especially around the statues and open courtyards. It’s worth allocating enough time for this place, as there’s a lot to see. A must-visit spiritual and cultural site in Ninh Binh, even on a rainy day.

reviews.author: DC
reviews.text: Bái Đính Pagoda is extraordinary, especially considering it’s only about 20 years old. The scale is immense, with huge parking areas and impressive structures, yet when we visited in late January 2026 it was surprisingly calm. Unless you struggle with long walks or are short on time, there’s really no need for the hop on hop off tickets. The cooler weather makes wandering the complex very pleasant. The Báo Thiên Stupa is a real highlight, offering beautiful views across the landscape, though the massive cement factory in the distance does distract slightly. Overall, a striking and memorable place to explore.

reviews.author: Hunter Reed
reviews.text: We had no idea what we were in for when we decided to show up here on our last day! We ended up spending about 5-6 hours walking around the complex getting awesome photos. We went on a day very few people were there. It felt very much like a convention center for Buddhism / cultural tourism… you can tell they are preparing the grounds to host thousands of people daily. We loved the ancient history part of the complex (the sites in the mountain). This may be a hot take, but I would recommend booking a night at the hotel in the complex so you can take your time exploring and finding the best photo spots! There are so many.

reviews.author: Thuha Ng
reviews.text: 🎺If you wanna seek somewhere peaceful in holidays, it is not the right place because it is really crowded those days. We were trying to find an old temple but it is hard to find. But the new temple was also a quite good place to visit for a whole day. Some place was still renovated or built now. Note that you should buy an electric car ticket to go around this area.

reviews.author: Verbena Bezbaruah
reviews.text: I was looking for some tranquility during my trip and a friend suggested I come here. This place is beautiful. It's a big complex so if you get tired of walking easily or have trouble navigating, take the shuttle service. I explored it for 3 hours and it was a soul calming experience overall. I've been to pagodas and temples before but this is unlike any I've seen.

reviews.author: sarita iyengar
reviews.text: The most serene pagoda we have been to. The old temple ( the steps to go up) totally worth the effort and so calm on top! Make sure to get the buggy ride (300k vnd per person, so worth it). We would love to stay there for 2 or 3 days to explore in full...highly recommend to visit as early in morning to beat the crowds.

reviews.author: L. S.
reviews.text: We visited this place on a weekday so there were not many visitors. We decided to walk (instead of booking the electric car) which I can recommend since the highlight was to walk through the beautiful landscape between the Pagoda and temples. This way you can enjoy the green surroundings, the quietness of the place and some fresh air. We did not enter each place but picked some highlights and enjoyed it. A good option to spend an afternoon.

reviews.author: Manh Dthe
reviews.text: Bai Dinh Pagoda is an impressive Buddhist architectural complex, located in a beautiful natural area. The pagoda is built on a large area, with many unique architectural works and exquisite decorations. The highlight of Bai Dinh Pagoda is the giant bell tower, where a large bell and a large bronze drum are hung. The pagoda also has a large worship area, where visitors can come to pray and worship. However, because the pagoda is too large, visitors should be prepared for walking and exploring. In addition, it is recommended to visit in the early morning or late afternoon to avoid crowds. Overall, Bai Dinh Pagoda is a great tourist destination for those who love culture and spirituality. If you have the opportunity to visit Ninh Binh, please visit Bai Dinh Pagoda!

reviews.author: Alice C
reviews.text: A very impressive pagoda, everything here is both massive and incredibly delicate. Highly suggest taking the ticket with electric car to get around. The complex is huge; even with the car, we spent over 3 hours exploring ( and we not even finish all the visit) because there is just so much to see. Also if you bought the combo ticket with the Trang A boat tour (the green wristband), you can get a 15-minute foot soak in a dedicated area! It was a short relax during our visit.

reviews.author: Adam Ding Media
reviews.text: Really beautiful Buddhist temple site. Multiple gates representing different deities and legends within the Buddhist context. Visited on a day with limited time, which was unfortunately. But definitely recommend allocating at least 3-4 hours to this place, with the tram service to transfer between zones quickly and easily, as the area is quite hilly. The highlight of this place is the Pagoda for me, for the majestic view across ninbh binh and the legends of Buddhist.

reviews.author: Dainis Daudiss
reviews.text: If you want to enjoy whole area I would suggest take hop on-off and then walk around and enjoy these amazing complex. To explore everything you will need 3-5 hours but it is worth. I think it is best to do in Ninh Binh after Trang An.

reviews.author: Adam Schroder
reviews.text: Perhaps I expected too much with such high reviews, but I found my experience to be pretty lackluster. The various temples and the large pagoda are all impressive on their own, but I found the holistic experience to be quite poor. To start, there is a lot of construction not only on the sites themselves (there were tools, and the smell of wet paint and lacquer at one temple). Instead of the peaceful serene silence atop the mountainside, one simply hears the clanging of gears and machines in the surrounding area. One must enter and exit the whole experience by going through gift shops hawking cheap plastic toys, bags, and statues. I walked away from the day trip having enjoyed seeing some sites, but not feeling any sort of positive spiritual or cultural takeaways. So is it worth it? I’d say the place still makes a great day trip, the sites are worth the entry price. Do the contents match the label on the box? I don’t think so. Just go in with eyes open on what to expect.

reviews.author: Kartik Godawat
reviews.text: Amazing place. Very beautifully maintained and peaceful. We took the electric caddy option but realised it's way more beautiful if you walk around instead. The old monastery short hike was great as well. Needs 4-5 hours to visit properly and soak-in the surroundings and the temple environment.

reviews.author: Remse Family
reviews.text: This place was actually quite amazing in it's scope. Definitely do the electric cart it is too vast to walk. Went with 2 kids . Cost 1.1m VND (about 65 AUD) for 2 adults and 2 kids. Worth it. Spent about 3 hours here. Get in early. The temples are ornate and grand on a scale where I actually said wow out loud a couple of times, I never do that.

reviews.author: David Kukla
reviews.text: Bái Đính Pagoda Complex – a return after 15 years We visited Bái Đính again (january 2026) after a 15-year gap. In the meantime, the former construction site has calmly transformed into a truly, almost comically massive complex. Monumental, unapologetic, and very clearly designed for mass tourism — and yet, somehow, it still has its charm. The corridors lined with arhats make for a surprisingly pleasant walk, the Buddha pavilions are genuinely impressive, and the 13-storey pagoda, nearly 100 meters high, is a real highlight. After a long time, this was the first viewpoint I genuinely enjoyed, rather than merely tolerated. Yes, it’s big. Yes, it’s ambitious. Yes, it occasionally feels like Buddhism met a megaproject consultant. But it works. I’ll happily come back in another 15 years. They’re still building — by then, it might even be finished.

reviews.author: Manasvi Balsara
reviews.text: Bai Dinh Pagoda is incredibly rich in history and truly impressive in scale. The complex is spread over a vast area, making it difficult to cover on foot. Electric cars are available to travel between different spots, and purchasing the ticket with the electric shuttle was absolutely worth it. Each pagoda has QR code scanners along with Wi-Fi access, allowing visitors to easily learn about the history and significance of the place. We were also warmly offered some of the food that had been offered to the deity, which was a very kind and touching gesture by the staff. The best time to visit is in the evening—you can explore the pagoda in daylight, and after sunset, witness the entire complex beautifully illuminated. Overall, it was a peaceful, well-organized, and memorable experience.

reviews.author: Livie G.
reviews.text: It seemed to me like Vatican but for Buddhists :) its very extensive, beautiful and impressive, its a lot to discover and for proper discovery you would need like almost whole day but you can do quicker more shallow tour in like 2-3hrs ...its very tourist friendly ...close to No.13 - herbal soak bath - is a great cafe not to be missed but many other refreshment stations and nice clean restrooms are all around the area. Definetely buy ticket option for 300k dong. It totally worths it and you ll appreciate taking a ride by buggy ...important info: when we visited THERE WAS NO RESTAURANT with warm meals at the visitors center...we expected there would be restaurant at such a big tourist spot but they only had filled dumplings:( if that happens to you and you are there hungry go to Family organic restaurant which is like 5 mins away by scooter...its very good and cheap meal...you can thank me later:)

reviews.author: Lydia Chu
reviews.text: Bái Đính Pagoda is an incredible place to visit and one of the highlights of Ninh Binh. The complex is huge with hundreds of Buddha statues lined along the walkways and several impressive temples to explore. The main pagoda is beautiful and the giant bronze Buddha is breathtaking to see in person. I went as part of a tour, but honestly it is not worth it. You can easily go on your own since the entrance fee is relatively cheap. There are many steps to climb but the views and the peaceful atmosphere make it worthwhile. It is a very spiritual and impressive site and definitely worth adding to your trip in Ninh Binh.

reviews.author: Ken Wong
reviews.text: Totally awesome to see ancient architecture so large and grand. The main temple is mostly made of wood. The pagoda is a stone structure with 2 elevators inside. This place should not be missed when one is visiting Ninh Binh. I spent over 3 hrs in this place.

reviews.author: Angeline Ismail
reviews.text: Would recommend going in the afternoon so you can end your journey when it gets dark and see the nice lights. Great place with little to no crowds during the time i went (late dec). I would recommend climbing the route to ancient pagoda, the road to the stairs was also very mysterious and nice especially when it gets darker

reviews.author: Mirko Krsic
reviews.text: Amazing experience. The temple complex is vast and impressive. There is so much to explore, starting from the Halls over the Ancient Pagoda and caves and finally the modern Pagoda, in which you can Take an Elevator to the top. One of the Highlights of my Vietnam Trip. Just one negative Point, figurung Out where to Go to actually get into the complex was quite confusing. You have to Go to the visitors Center which is located in the far Side of the parking area. Basically away from the temples. There you get into an EV and they Drive you back through the parking area to the temples.

reviews.author: Artur Pytel
reviews.text: This Pagoda has the tallest tower and largest gold coated Buddha statue in Southeast Asia. Place is huge, you need mimimum 3 hours if not more to see most of the things. It's not worth to go to point 27, the rest is very interesting and worth seeing. There's an elevator in the tower so it's very easy to go to the top, to 12th floor. The view of the entire complex is magnificent.

reviews.author: valentina gonzalez torres
reviews.text: Beautiful place. Nice experience. You have 3 ticket options, the three offers you the electric car to take you to the starting point of the circuit and bring you back when you finish (DON’T lose the ticket paper because you have to deliver it to get on the electric car and get to the departure point). It took us 3 hours to go through it, taking photos and videos. What I can highlight as something that is missing is: hydration points, because at 5 pm the coffee place was already closed and the one near the pagoda was also already closed. The other thing is the lack of mastery of basic English, because sometimes with signs they are not good saying that “it is closed” or “do not go there” or whatever. I did not see anywhere something safety point.

reviews.author: Katleen Verloo
reviews.text: Beautifully laid out and the lighting at nights is astonishing. This temple complex is not very old though and entry fees are steep compared to other temples so it feels a bit as if you're paying it back in a way as a tourist. Very slippery when wet and not a lot of "functional" light at nightfall so beware.

reviews.author: Jan Židek
reviews.text: If you have time, it's worth it. Go for option 2. for 150k. It includes trasfer there and back in an electric car + you can get to the top of the 99m high pagoda (elevator) which will give you a nice view. Shuttle bus within the park (option 3 and 4) are really not necessary, you can walk it yourself. I spent almost two hours in the park and it was more than enough.

reviews.author: Gabriel Radu
reviews.text: If you don’t do your research in advance, prepared to be surprised by this sprawling complex spanning hundreds of acres of land. There’s ancient stone temples over 1000 years old and a newer temple complex completed fairly recently. A visit will take easily 2 hours or more, but it’s the most relaxed, tranquil setting with lush gardens.

reviews.author: Jakub Kozak
reviews.text: Shrine complex with a lot of to see. Some of the shrines and statues inside are really impressive as well as tall stupa. Worth visiting if you have a plenty of time in are however there are better way to spend time if you are there only for a few days.

reviews.author: Chung Karen
reviews.text: We joined a day tour from Hanoi with this as 1st visiting points after 2 hours drive. The place is big and we took electric car to the main temples where here is great gold plated Buddha.
